message:
🐛 Fix 22 missions: wrong Helm repos, fabricated paths, CLI misrepresentation (#1468)

Wrong/fabricated Helm repos (9 missions → real install methods):
- opentofu: CLI install (was using krateo.io chart)
- parsec: binary install (was using fabricated chart URL)
- knative-eventing: kubectl apply from releases (was using personal GH pages)
- operator-framework: OLM install script (was using ReadTheDocs URL)
- trickster: kubectl deploy (was using appscode chart)
- envoy: Docker/kubectl (was using generic aggregator)
- carina: kubectl apply from releases (was using personal GH pages)
- kubevirt: kubectl apply from releases (was using third-party chart)
- kubeflow: kustomize (was using Alauda fork chart)

Fabricated file paths (13 missions → inline YAML or real URLs):
- aeraki-mesh, backstage, bfe, bpfman, cdk8s, confidential-containers,
  continuous-optimization, dalec, keylime, OPA, score, slimfaas, tokenetes

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,84 +11,53 @@
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Clone the Backstage repository",
-        "description": "Start by cloning the Backstage repository to your local machine. This will allow you to customize your Backstage instance as needed.\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/backstage/backstage.git\ncd backstage\n```"
+        "title": "Install Backstage via Helm",
+        "description": "Add the Backstage Helm chart repository and install:\n```bash\nhelm repo add backstage https://backstage.github.io/charts\nhelm repo update\nkubectl create namespace backstage\nhelm install backstage backstage/backstage --namespace backstage --version 2.4.0\n```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install dependencies",
-        "description": "Install the necessary dependencies using Yarn. This step ensures that all required packages are available for your Backstage instance.\n```bash\nyarn install\n```"
+        "title": "Configure the app-config",
+        "description": "Create a ConfigMap with your Backstage configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -n backstage -f - << 'EOF'\napiVersion: v1\nkind: ConfigMap\nmetadata:\n  name: backstage-app-config\ndata:\n  app-config.yaml: |\n    app:\n      title: My Backstage\n      baseUrl: http://localhost:7007\n    backend:\n      baseUrl: http://localhost:7007\n      database:\n        client: better-sqlite3\n        connection: ':memory:'\nEOF\n```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Build the Backstage application",
-        "description": "Build the Backstage application to prepare it for deployment. This step compiles the application and prepares it for running in a Kubernetes environment.\n```bash\nyarn build\n```"
+        "title": "Access the Backstage UI",
+        "description": "Port-forward to access the Backstage dashboard:\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/backstage -n backstage 7007:7007\n```\nOpen http://localhost:7007"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Deploy Backstage to Kubernetes",

+        "title": "Verify the installation",

-        "title": "Clean up associated resources",
-        "description": "Delete any associated services and persistent volume claims (PVCs) created during the installation. Replace `<namespace>` with your chosen namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl delete service backstage --namespace <namespace>\nkubectl delete pvc --all --namespace <namespace>\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Remove the namespace",
-        "description": "If you created a new namespace for Backstage, you can delete it to clean up your Kubernetes environment. Replace `<namespace>` with your chosen namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace <namespace>\n```"
+        "title": "Remove Backstage",
+        "description": "```bash\nhelm uninstall backstage -n backstage\nkubectl delete namespace backstage\n```"
       }
     ],
     "upgrade": [
       {
-        "title": "Backup current state",
-        "description": "Before upgrading, back up your current Backstage configuration and data. This ensures you can restore your system if needed.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace <namespace> -o yaml > backup-backstage.yaml\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Pull the latest changes",
-        "description": "Fetch the latest changes from the Backstage repository to get the newest version.\n```bash\ngit pull origin main\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Rebuild the Backstage application",

-      },

-        "title": "Redeploy Backstage",

-      {
-        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
-        "description": "Check the status of the Backstage pods to ensure they are running the new version correctly. Replace `<namespace>` with your chosen namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace <namespace>\n```"
+        "title": "Upgrade via Helm",
+        "description": "```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade backstage backstage/backstage --namespace backstage\n```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
-        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
-        "description": "If your Backstage pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace <namespace>\n```"
+        "title": "Backstage pod not starting",
+        "description": "```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=backstage -n backstage\n```\nCommon issue: missing app-config.yaml configuration."
       },
       {
-        "title": "Backstage not accessible",

-        "title": "Error in application logs",
-        "description": "If you encounter errors in the application, check the logs for more details.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace <namespace>\n```"
+        "title": "Plugin loading errors",
+        "description": "Check the Backstage container logs for missing dependencies or configuration."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of Backstage will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the Backstage interface through the configured service. Ensure that resource limits are set appropriately for optimal performance.",
+      "summary": "Backstage developer portal is installed and accessible via the web UI.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "git clone https://github.com/backstage/backstage.git\ncd backstage",
-        "yarn install",
-        "kubectl apply -f k8s/ --namespace <namespace> --create-namespace",
-        "kubectl get pods --namespace <namespace>",
-        "kubectl edit deployment backstage --namespace <namespace>\n# Add resource limits under spec.containers:\n# resources:\n#   limits:\n#     cpu: \"500m\"\n#     memory: \"512Mi\""
+        "helm install backstage backstage/backstage --namespace backstage",
+        "kubectl port-forward svc/backstage -n backstage 7007:7007"
       ]
     }
   },

+        "description": "Run Envoy as a standalone proxy:
```bash

```
Or install the binary:
```bash
# macOS
brew install envoy

# Linux (from GetEnvoy)
curl -L https://func-e.io/install.sh | bash -s -- -b /usr/local/bin
func-e run --version
```"

-        "title": "Install Envoy using Helm",

helm install envoy envoy/envoy --namespace envoy --create-namespace --version 1.5.0
```"
+        "title": "Deploy Envoy on Kubernetes",
+        "description": "Deploy Envoy as a sidecar or standalone proxy:
```bash
kubectl create namespace envoy
kubectl apply -n envoy -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: envoy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: envoy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: envoy
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: envoy
        image: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3
        ports:
        - containerPort: 10000
        - containerPort: 9901
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: envoy
spec:
  selector:
    app: envoy
  ports:
  - name: proxy
    port: 10000
  - name: admin
    port: 9901
EOF
```"
       },

```bash

# Kubernetes
kubectl set image deployment/envoy envoy=envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3 -n envoy
```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [

     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Install Gatekeeper via Helm",
-        "description": "Add the Gatekeeper Helm chart repository and install Gatekeeper into the gatekeeper-system namespace:
```bash
helm repo add gatekeeper https://open-policy-agent.github.io/gatekeeper/charts
helm install gatekeeper/gatekeeper --name-template=gatekeeper --namespace gatekeeper-system --create-namespace
```
Alternatively, install via kubectl apply:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-policy-agent/gatekeeper/v3.21.1/deploy/gatekeeper.yaml
```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the installation",
-        "description": "Check that the Gatekeeper controller and audit pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n gatekeeper-system
```
You should see gatekeeper-controller-manager and gatekeeper-audit pods in Running state."
+        "title": "Install OPA Gatekeeper via Helm",
+        "description": "Add the Gatekeeper Helm repository and install:
```bash
helm repo add gatekeeper https://open-policy-agent.github.io/gatekeeper/charts
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace gatekeeper-system
helm install gatekeeper gatekeeper/gatekeeper --namespace gatekeeper-system --version 3.18.2
```"
       },
       {
         "title": "Create a constraint template",
-        "description": "Define a constraint template that specifies the policy logic in Rego. For example, to require all namespaces to have a specific label:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-policy-agent/gatekeeper-library/master/library/general/requiredlabels/template.yaml
```
Verify the template was created:
```bash
kubectl get constrainttemplates
```"
+        "description": "Define a policy that requires labels on namespaces:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: templates.gatekeeper.sh/v1
kind: ConstraintTemplate
metadata:
  name: k8srequiredlabels
spec:
  crd:
    spec:
      names:
        kind: K8sRequiredLabels
      validation:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            labels:
              type: array
              items:
                type: string
  targets:
    - target: admission.k8s.gatekeeper.sh
      rego: |
        package k8srequiredlabels
        violation[{"msg": msg}] {
          provided := {l | input.review.object.metadata.labels[l]}
          required := {l | l := input.parameters.labels[_]}
          missing := required - provided
          count(missing) > 0
          msg := sprintf("Missing required labels: %v", [missing])
        }
EOF
```"

-    "description": "Tokenetes is an open source Transaction Tokens (TraTs) Service — an implementation of the IETF OAuth Transaction Tokens draft specification. A complete installation involves three phases: tconfigd, the Tokenetes service, and Tokenetes agents (sidecars for TraT verification).",
+    "description": "Tokenetes is an open source Transaction Tokens (TraTs) Service — an implementation of the IETF OAuth Transaction Tokens draft specification. It enables secure propagation of identity and context across microservices via transaction tokens. Tokenetes uses SPIRE for workload identity and requires tconfigd for managing TraT configurations. A complete installation involves three phases: tconfigd, the Tokenetes service, and Tokenetes agents (sidecars for TraT verification).",
     "type": "deploy",

       {
-        "title": "Set up SPIRE for Tokenetes",
-        "description": "SPIRE must be running in your Kubernetes cluster before installing Tokenetes components. Configure the SPIRE server with the k8sbundle notifier plugin and ensure the SPIRE ClusterRole has the required permissions.

**1. Add the k8sbundle notifier to your SPIRE server configuration:**
```yaml
Notifier "k8sbundle" {
    plugin_data {
        webhook_label = "tokenetes.io/webhook"
    }
}
```

**2. Ensure the SPIRE server ClusterRole includes webhook permissions:**
```yaml
- apiGroups: ["admissionregistration.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["mutatingwebhookconfigurations", "validatingwebhookconfigurations"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "patch", "watch"]
```

**3. Register tconfigd with SPIRE:**
```bash
kubectl exec -n spire spire-server-0 -- \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server entry create \
    --dns tconfigd.tokenetes-system.svc \
    -spiffeID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/tconfigd \
    -parentID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/ns/spire/sa/spire-agent \
    -selector k8s:ns:tokenetes-system \
    -selector k8s:sa:tconfigd-service-account
```

**4. Register the Tokenetes service with SPIRE:**
```bash
kubectl exec -n spire spire-server-0 -- \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server entry create \
    -spiffeID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/tokenetes \
    -parentID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/ns/spire/sa/spire-agent \
    -selector k8s:ns:<your-namespace> \
    -selector k8s:sa:tokenetes-service-account
```

**5. Register each microservice that will verify TraTs:**
```bash
kubectl exec -n spire spire-server-0 -- \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server entry create \
    -spiffeID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/<service-name> \
    -parentID spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/ns/spire/sa/spire-agent \
    -selector k8s:ns:<your-namespace> \
    -selector k8s:sa:<service-account-name>
```
Repeat for each microservice in your application."
+        "title": "Install SPIRE (prerequisite)",
+        "description": "Tokenetes requires SPIRE for identity attestation. Install SPIRE first:
```bash
helm repo add spiffe https://spiffe.github.io/helm-charts-hardened/
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace spire
helm install spire spiffe/spire --namespace spire
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Install tconfigd",
-        "description": "tconfigd manages Transaction Token (TraT) configurations as Kubernetes custom resources. It must be running before deploying the Tokenetes service.

**1. Clone and navigate to the installation directory:**
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tokenetes/tconfigd.git
cd tconfigd/installation
```

**2. Edit `config.yaml` with your environment settings:**
```yaml
enableTratInterception: "true"          # "true" for interception mode, "false" for delegation mode
spireAgentHostDir: "/run/spire/sockets" # Path to SPIRE agent socket directory
tokenetesSpiffeId: "spiffe://<your-trust-domain>/tokenetes"  # SPIFFE ID of the Tokenetes service
agentApiPort: "9030"                    # Agent API port (used in delegation mode)
agentInterceptorPort: "9050"            # Agent interceptor port (used in interception mode)
```

**3. Run the install script:**
```bash
./install.sh
```

**4. Verify tconfigd is running:**
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tokenetes-system
```
You should see the tconfigd pod in `Running` state."

```"

-        "title": "Configure the Tokenetes manifests",
-        "description": "Before deploying, update the Kubernetes manifest files in the `kubernetes/` directory with your environment-specific values:

1. Replace `<your-namespace>` with your target namespace in all YAML files
2. Replace `<your-trust-domain>` with your SPIRE trust domain
3. Update the SPIRE agent socket path in `tokenetes-deployment.yaml` if your SPIRE agent socket is not at the default location (`/run/spire/sockets`)

```bash
# Create the target namespace
kubectl create namespace <your-namespace>
```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Deploy the Tokenetes service",
-        "description": "Apply the Kubernetes manifests from the `kubernetes/` directory in order:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service-account.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-deployment.yaml
kubectl apply -f kubernetes/tokenetes-service.yaml
```
Note: The Tokenetes service is deployed per application namespace, not cluster-wide. Repeat for each namespace that needs TraT support."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Set up Tokenetes Agents",
-        "description": "Tokenetes agents are sidecar containers injected into your application pods to handle TraT verification. tconfigd's mutating webhook automatically injects the agent sidecar when properly annotated.

**1. Label your application namespace to enable agent injection:**
```bash
kubectl label namespace <your-namespace> tokenetes-enabled=true
```

**2. Add annotations to your application deployments:**
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: <your-service>
  namespace: <your-namespace>
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        tokenetes/inject-sidecar: "true"        # Enable agent sidecar injection
        tokenetes/agent-mode: "interception"     # "interception" or "delegation"
        tokenetes/service-port: "<port-number>"  # Required for interception mode
```

**3. Restart your deployments to trigger injection:**
```bash
kubectl rollout restart deployment <your-service> -n <your-namespace>
```

**Operating modes:**
- **Interception mode**: The agent transparently intercepts incoming requests, extracts the TraT from the `Txn-Token` header, and verifies it before forwarding to your microservice. Requires the `tokenetes/service-port` annotation.
- **Delegation mode**: Requests are not intercepted. Your microservice explicitly calls the agent's local API at `POST http://localhost:9030/verify-trat` to verify TraTs."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Write TraT resources for your APIs",
-        "description": "Define Transaction Token (TraT) custom resources that describe the authorization context for each of your application's external APIs.

**1. Create a TraT resource for each API endpoint:**
```yaml
apiVersion: tokenetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: TraT
metadata:
  name: <trat-name>
  namespace: <your-namespace>
spec:
  path: "api/path/{#paramName}"        # API path with path variables
  method: "POST"                        # HTTP method
  purp: "<purpose>"                     # TraT purpose
  azdMapping:                           # Authorization details mapping
    fieldName:
      required: true
      value: "${body.field}"            # Sources: ${body.*}, ${header.*}, ${queryParameters.*}, ${variable_name}
  services:                             # Internal services that process this API
    - name: <service-name>
```

**2. Optionally create TraTExclusion resources to exclude endpoints (e.g., health checks):**
```yaml
apiVersion: tokenetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: TraTExclusion
metadata:
  name: <exclusion-name>
  namespace: <your-namespace>
spec:
  service: <service-name>
  endpoints:
    - path: "/health"
      method: "GET"
```

**3. Apply the resources:**
```bash
kubectl apply -f <trat-file>.yaml
kubectl apply -f <trat-exclusion-file>.yaml
```"
+        "title": "Configure token exchange",

```bash
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: tokenetes.otterize.com/v1alpha1
kind: TRAT
metadata:
  name: example-trat
  namespace: tokenetes
spec:
  serviceAccountName: my-service
  audiences:
    - my-api
EOF
```"

```
Agent-injected pods will show `2/2` in the READY column, indicating both the application container and the Tokenetes agent sidecar are running.

kubectl exec -n spire spire-server-0 -- \

```
Confirm entries exist for tconfigd, the Tokenetes service, and each registered microservice."

```bash

kubectl apply -n trickster -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: trickster
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: trickster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: trickster
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: trickster
        image: trickstercache/trickster:2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8480
        - containerPort: 8481
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: trickster
spec:
  selector:
    app: trickster
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 8480
    targetPort: 8480
  - name: metrics
    port: 8481
    targetPort: 8481
EOF
```"

EOF
